title: digitaria marginata var. fimbriata
intro: Digitaria marginata var. fimbriata, also known as finger grass, is a type of grass used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. It is valued for its health benefits, which include treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as wounds and skin infections. The herbal preparations of finger grass include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
